blocks.body: Separable and Inseparable Phrasal Verbs helps you place the object correctly and avoid pronoun mistakes such as turn it off.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"With phrasal verbs, you need to know not only the meaning but also where the object goes. This is where many common pronoun mistakes appear.","rules":[{"label":"Separable","color":"emerald","text":"With separable phrasal verbs, a noun object can often go before or after the particle.","example":"turn off the light / turn the light off"},{"label":"Pronouns","color":"blue","text":"If the object is a pronoun, it usually goes inside the separable phrasal verb.","example":"turn it off / pick them up"},{"label":"Inseparable","color":"amber","text":"With inseparable phrasal verbs, the object cannot split the expression and must come after it.","example":"look after the child / run into an old friend"}]}

blocks.heading: 1. Core idea and form
blocks.body:
The simplest approach is to ask two questions: Can the expression be split? and What happens if the object is a pronoun?

  • Separable: turn off, pick up, put on, take off.
  • Inseparable: look after, run into, come across, deal with.
  • In three-part verbs such as put up with or get on with, the object also usually stays after the full expression.

blocks.heading: 2. Main uses and examples
blocks.body:
TypeCorrect patternExample
separable + noun objectverb + object + particle / verb + particle + objectPick up the phone. / Pick the phone up.
separable + pronounverb + pronoun + particlePick it up.
inseparableverb + particle + objectLook after your brother.
three-part verbverb + particle + preposition + objectWe need to put up with the noise.

blocks.heading: 3. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: turn off it.
    Better: turn it off.
  • Mistake: look the baby after.
    Better: look after the baby, because it is an inseparable phrasal verb.
  • Mistake: applying the separable rule to every phrasal verb. Not every expression can be split.
  • Mistake: forgetting that long noun objects often sound more natural after the particle: write down the number from the message.

blocks.heading: 4. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• Pronoun + separable phrasal verb = pronoun in the middle: pick it up.
  • Inseparable phrasal verb = do not split the expression: look after her.
  • If you are unsure, check the pattern in a dictionary together with an example sentence.
